sampler.mechanism: Groups question bank rows by concept; loops for (const [concept, variants] of byConcept.entries()); picks ONE random unused variant per concept; shuffles order.
sampler.note: Expanding seed to 8 concepts × 3 variants yields 8 questions per attempt without sampler rewrite. Current banks with 4 concepts still yield 4 until seed is expanded (owned by content lanes / LANE-S01-P5).

ui.intro_copy: Una pregunta por concepto (N del banco; V3 full = 8)

admin.note: Admin renders variantSeed and per-answer concept dynamically; no fixed 4-question assumption.

residual_risks.description: 8-question exams increase scroll/cognitive load vs 4. Mobile: long vertical list of MC cards; no sticky progress/index or section pagination yet.

Pass is percentage-based (70%), so it scales for any N. With N=8, pass = ≥6/8 correct; with N=4, ≥3/4. No integer-rounding edge at 70% for 8 (75% or 62.5%→63). With N that cannot hit exactly 70%, nearest-above may feel strict (e.g. N=3: 2/3=67 fails, need 3/3).
Until LANE-S01-P5 / content lanes expand question bank to 8 concepts, runtime still serves 4 questions for sections with only 4 concepts in DB. Platform is ready; content is the gate.
submit divides by detailedAnswers.length; empty answers array would yield NaN score. UI requires answering all questions before submit, so unlikely in normal flow.
With 3 variants and 3 attempts, anti-reuse works. After all variants used, sampler resets to full set. If a concept has fewer than 3 variants, reuse happens earlier.
README.md / AGENT_STATE.md still mention 4 questions / 4 concepts historically; not updated in this lane (docs outside platform UI scope).
